tokens: LM Crew: (Sung with occasionally successful harmony) Good morning to you; Good morning to you; Good morning, Dear Gordy, Good morning to you.
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Good morning, Challenger and thank you for the vocal rendition from the Moon, there.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Well, we thank you for your kind music.
We wanted to let you know we were thinking about you this morning, Gordy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You just beat us to it, but...
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: ...we decided to play it anyway, because it's such a pretty selection.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I think it's very apropos at the moment.
I guess I can just wait for "Hail Purdue," huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, just stand by, you'll probably hear it before you get back.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Hey, we've been stirring for about 15 or 20 minutes.
We're in the midst of a nice hamburger omelet (chuckle) and assorted accessories.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: As a matter of fact, it's all over us.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And if you'll give me 5 minutes, I'll be ready to go on the PGNS, unless you want to start it sooner.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay...No hurry, finish up and get cleaned up there.
And the only change...We have some change in the timing as far as the checklist so we can gain back the time we're behind now, which is actually less than an hour.
And the only hardware change other than deletions is that we'd like you to leave Demand Reg A closed at all times.
So whenever you come across a place that says Open at Cabin or Egress, we'd like you to leave it Closed.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And, Gordy, could I have a quick status report on America and Challenger?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: America is just as good as gold, just like always.
Ron got off the trim burn on the back side followed by a good plane change on the front side.
In fact the G&N cut-off was a tenth (of a foot per second) and - or less - in all axes.
Didn't even need to trim it.
So, he's in about a 62-1/2 circular (orbit), I believe, and waiting for you to come up and join him.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Pause) Okay, America's consumables are great and so are yours.
There is a possibility we may have to switch to Ascent Water (tank) just prior to lift-off.
Everything really is in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: In honor of one of your comm handovers last night, and in the tradition of Apollo 8, I've got a paraphrase of a familiar poem for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gordo, that was the first time I heard that and I got to say that is beautiful!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Did the LMP get any sleep or did he spend all night composing that?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: People always said we ought to have a poet in space.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think we've made it yet.
(Answering Fullerton) No, for some reason I really woke up with one of your handovers last night, and that was how I went back to sleep.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: One update for the post-sleep procedure.
I understand you brought in the LMP's camera, and we want to be sure you get that into the
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: It's already in the jett bag, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, Gordy, you might make some notes that before I put it in there (in the ETB at about 174:31:44), I took a black-and-white window pan with mag Nancy (Apollo magazine 143).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gordo, on Reg A, you interpret that is just a small leak by the reg?
That it would be usable, if you had to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's a small, slow leak and it is usable if needed.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: In summing up so far, I think that's all the system anomalies we got, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's all I can think of at the moment.
I do have revised times for the rest of the Lunar Surface Checklist which, at a convenient time, I can give to you, so that you'll have a how-goes-it as you go on down the line here.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Why don't you give them to us now, Gordy?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, (on page) 7-9, LGC/IMU Power-up, change that time from 183:04 to 184:10.
The eat period time is now 184:20.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, we're going to delete everything from Verb 95 through P00 on P22.
That's the center of the page.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's affirmative and then the next page, "cabin prep for ascent" is 186:45, and instead of 39 minutes we're going to have you do that in 3 minutes.
And you should have a little more time than that, since you got up early.
That puts you right back with timeline, at lift-off minus 1 hour and 15, at 186:48.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I guess we'll just have to work that in with the cabin cleanup time.
We had deleted that whole time block but you do have to catch those procedures.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, and then the times on the top of
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We might...There's a couple more changes coming at me here but let me make sure I got them straight and I'll call you later.
Go ahead with whatever you were doing.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Coughs) Gordy, if I get ready for the "LGC/IMU power-up", I'll give you a call even though it comes earlier; but I'd like to get that started, as soon as we're ready to start it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Gordy, as far as the food, medication, and sleep goes...(We took) no medication.
Gene had 5 hours of good sleep, I had probably around my usual 6, in spite of my poetic inclinations.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the food...We continue to do well, I believe.
We've eaten a wet pack apiece last night.
We've eaten our scrambled eggs and sausage yesterday, and we've run out of juices and tea.
And which is somewhat of an inconvenience I might say.
And if you've got any specific questions, I can fill you in on it; but, I think, foodwise we've done pretty well.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Can you give us a feel on the fluid intake?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Well, that's what I was trying to do when I said we'd drunk all the juices and tea.
Plus, Gene, in particular, takes water from the hose.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Gordy, it's PGNS (Primary Guidance and Navigation System) power up time.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I've got a couple more items to completely clean up the checklist whenever you can get to that.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'll let Jack give you a call.
I'm going to start on the PGNS, if you're ready.
(No answer; Long pause) Gordy, you ready for the PGNS?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's affirmative, finally got you a Go to start it as per checklist.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay there's the Pro; I did get the Restart.
(Pause) I got the No Att, the DAP, Gimbal Lock light, and a Program Alarm light's on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The gimbal operate breaker is Closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll stand by for 90 seconds (until the corresponding lights go out).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And we're ready for a Power Amp, Primary; and High Bit Rate.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordo, self-test is complete and it's Go; and I'll give you an E(rasable)-memory dump.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we're ready, standing by for it.
And while it's coming, I could give you the update for the next page.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, it's coming at you and you can go with the updates on the next page.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And then we'd like you to delete all the steps below that lift-off time, from the A/T-3 data star info box through the Verb 32 after "for the remaining data stars" and after last star.
In other words, just delete all between the box and up to circuit breaker AOT Lamp, Open.
And, of course, there'll be no P22 time, over.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Delete all the data star information between the start of that box and, "after last star, Verb 34 Enter, 00 Enter."
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's correct and then turn to page
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We've deleted everything but the last three lines on that page.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's all we got for you, and we have the E-MOD dump okay.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'll get an answer for that, and I think we'll be coming with the up-link here shortly.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Gene, no time constraint on that AOT breaker; except, if it (the telescope sight)'s foggy, it hasn't been in long enough.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You have a state vector, a time increment, and an RLS.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Well, it (the computer) likes the command module's orbit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Doesn't look like the Challenger shifted around much in the last 3 days.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Well, at least it always settles back to the same spot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Say, Gordy, are we clear to jettison the Buddy SLSS bag, now?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've determined you won't need it for rock stowage.
And along the same line, John Young suggested that you might check your checklist pockets on your suits, when you get to that point.
You might want to take those off and discard them, if that's the case, so that it won't fly up and get you at insertion.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Yeah, we already did that, Gordy.
They were a mess, and we took them off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're going to take time out for about 15 or 20 minutes of mandatory housekeeping here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I just went off biomed briefly.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And Gene will be on biomed shortly.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How do you read biomed and voice?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're copying your ticker (that is, his heart beat) loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You ought to have me all back up on the comm and biomed now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're ready to don the helmets and gloves (as per
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're going to press on, but don't let us miss 185:58 for the VHF check (with Ron Evans), will you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're getting close to the bottom on descent water.
We're expecting to have to switch to ascent water sometime in the next hour.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I cleaned them up for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and loud and clear on VOX.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Well, the (LM data) recorder's on, but I don't think it's going to work.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See if I've got anymore juice in here (in the suit drink dispenser).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; my right glove is on and locked.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, I just came to the end of my grease.
Probably a good time, because if everything goes the way it's supposed to, I shouldn't put on these EV gloves anymore.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't you use that in your IV (gloves)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: No, I don't need it with my IV.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Mine are all on and verified.
(Pause) Okay, we got 8 minutes to the comm check, so let's press on.
(Pause) Hate to get in the middle of the integrity check...Oh, we can do it.
Let me know when you (garbled) with your glove.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I did once, Gene, and I...Didn't help, and I started to run out of air.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: "Suit shall not be maintained at elevated pressure greater than 5 minutes." Okay;
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; we will leave Pressure Reg A, Close.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Pressure Reg B, Direct O2, and we'll go up to 3.7 or 4.0 on the cuff gauge, and then you want to go to Egress on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; you can go on up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; the suit circuit's coming up.
(Long Pause) Okay; suit circuit's about (3.)72 now.
(Cuff pressure gauge) should be coming off the peg shortly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) You can stop it on your cuff gauge...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) My suit's going to be good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Suit circuit pressure decays to 4.8." Coming down.
The next thing is depress, but before we do that, I think we ought to get this VHF check.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) I got a scratch on my helmet!
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We're going to configure for the VHF here.
Standing by for a call from the CMP.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; sounds like a good idea.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And we'll press on after that.
Jack, you better make sure your volume's up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're A, Off and Receive, B on the Audio.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, what do we do once we hear him?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Probably answer him is the first thing.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want A, Transmitter, Voice/Range, then A to T/R, and A Transmitter, Off...after conversation.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: So as soon as you hear him, you want the VHF A Transmitter to Voice/Range.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) He's going to try to lock up on us I guess, huh?
(Pause) They've got us VHF Ranging, so we'll have to be quiet once we get established.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's like home in these suits.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Challenger, about 30 seconds to the comm check.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Hearing Evans VHF carrier as he comes over their horizon) There he is.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; I'm going to go Voice/Ranging, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Wait...No, wait until he gives us the call.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, Houston; we're reading CMP on VOX.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Captain America, this is Challenger.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You must have turned away from your mike.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Standing by now for you and going to do a little landmark tracking when I go over this time.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Ron, are you going to do any ranging at all?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, we got a beautiful bird down here.
We'll see you up there shortly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you're kind of fading out a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Hang in there and keep your (docking) probe extended!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; we're going to go VHF, Off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll see you just prior to lift-off, then.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: That's all you need to do.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...you have a Go from us for depress.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You might be able to (see the CSM as it goes overhead).
He'll be well sunlit up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; we have a Go for depress, Jack.
On (Circuit Breaker Panel) 16, ECS Cabin Repress (circuit breaker), Open.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you watch your gauge, and I'm going to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Faintly) I guess I can turn my VHF off if their's is off.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: I'm going to open the forward dump to Auto to 3.5.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: "Verify suit circuit lockup at (greater than) 4.3 and decaying."
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, man, I got to go get that Water Sep(arator) Select.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got longer arms, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I can reach back in there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If you can get back there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, yeah, we got a decay in the suit loop.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay; verify suit circuit lock's up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you're at 0.7 in the cabin and 4.1 in the suits.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; let me see if I can jar it (the hatch).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; I guess we're Go for EVA-4.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, man, I got it open.
If I could bleed that pressure.
Let me hold it here for a minute.
(Breathing heavily, but chuckling) There's a lot of psi on that hatch.
Let me go to Auto here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Save our (surface) checklist (from blowing out the hatch).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's Auto, and the lock/lock is on.
(Pause) Hatch is open all the way.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why is that still waving in the breeze?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a solar wind in here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Seem's to still be a little breeze going out the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: All the numbers look good here, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: 3-point kick (a reference to a 'field goal' in American football).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All clear of the Ascent Stage.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston, joking) Are we Stay NoStay for hatch closure?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You have a Go for closing the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Boy, is it easy to get around in here without a PLSS on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; on (circuit breaker panel) 16, Cabin Repress, Closed?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the hatch looked clear when I sealed it.
Clear when I closed the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And the cabin is coming up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Sound of repress begins) Okay; cabin is increasing, and you can go to Cabin on Pressure Reg B (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Cabin warning light will go off here shortly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're just about to go through 50 percent, Descent O2 (remaining).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Next thing we'll do when it stabilizes, we'll doff our gloves, doff our helmets...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me watch that pressure a minute.
(Pause) Well, it looks like it's stable at five.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Physics says it should have been.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Not really, since we do that with our PLSS and everything else.
(Pause) Jack, I'd wrap that thing around it like (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Stow them on the engine cover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Before you stow the right-hand (that is, Jack's) OPS on the floor, you need to attach the floor hole cover.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay; we're...Jack's down there cleaning up the floor now, and I'm working on the visors.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We have a copy of an update on the rock stowage that was given to you last night.
We're not sure if they read one part of it to you, and that was concerning using some contingency webbing to reinforce the tiedown of the bag that goes on the Z-27 bulkhead.
If you did get that update, forget it.
We do not need any extra tiedown other than the normal tiedown.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: That's for the Buddy SLSS bag, I guess, and we did not get it (the update).
But we haven't quite stowed that yet anyway, so we will forget it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Are the Rover batteries still alive?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'll check with the Backroom, here.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'm standing by with a PAD any time you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'll be ready in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Gordy; go with the Ascent PAD.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This is the Direct Ascent PAD.
Tig (Ignition Time) is 188:01:35.93; Noun 76 is 55408, 00320, minus 0005; DEDA 047 is plus 37430, minus 72507, plus 58669; plus 56907, plus 00320, plus 05359; and TPI time is 188:55:5700.
LM weight 10900 (pounds) ; and HA is 629; HP 622.
One remark; your Tig for one rev late is 190:00:18.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Remark: one rev late Tig is 190:00:18.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, that was a good readback.
Now I have a Co-elliptic Ascent PAD.
Rest of the PAD down to LM weight is NA.
(NA = not applicable) Your LM weight is 10900, and the HA and HP are NA.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordy, co-elliptic readback: 188:04:14.00; 55390, 00380, minus 0005; plus 37430; minus 72507; 58630; 56907; 00380; rest of PAD is NA except for LM weight, 10900.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Turn to page 10 (of the LM Data Book); I have the CSI PAD, co-elliptic.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And, Gordy, I'm starting to pick up the breakers on panel 11.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Noun 81 is 0539; and Delta-VY is a plus all balls (that is, all zeros).
373; 05419, 06550; plus 0539; plus all balls; and plus 0012.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: CSI PAD: 189:01:53.81; 190:55 all zeros; plus 0539; plus all zeros; 05419; 06550; plus 0539; plus all zeros; plus 0012.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's all I have for you.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Gordo, we're on the top of (page) 8-4.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gordy, are you ready for
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I've got both shaft and trunnion and
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: On the radar test, everything is Go.
The only anomaly is the one I just reported.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: In 20 seconds here, on the hour even, we'll have a site handover to Goldstone (from
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You can go ahead and park the radar at 0 and 30.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: It's going there right now, as a matter of fact.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: When you parked it there, we saw it go to the proper places.
From all our indications, the interface between the radar and the PGNS is okay.
And our best guess is some kind of self-test problem.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'm going to go to Auto (tracking) on the S-band (the
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And go ahead with the check.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I'll give you a call before I fire, but we're in the process of getting ready for the RCS.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Gordy, the AGS check looked good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here we go on the PGNS, Gordy.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, you've got Data and P00.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'll give you a vector and zero the pos/neg cells.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We'd like you to put the Ascent Batteries, On, according to the procedures on the next page, 8-8, just a little early because of pre-conditioning noticed before descent.
And we checked your RGA's during the hot fire, and your hot fire itself, and they both looked good.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And, Challenger, it's your computer now.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Gordo, did you ever get any word on the Rover batteries?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I haven't heard on that one.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I just wondered whether those things were going to be working back there (to give TV of lift-off).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got no reason to believe they won't.
When you get down to parking the rendezvous radar antenna after this P57 (platform alignment program), give me a call.
We're going to change the parking position.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Pause) Why don't you just give me the numbers now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Do as the checklist shows (bottom left on 8-8), except do a Verb 41 Noun 72 to 0 and 30 degrees.
Go to Slew, and then delete the "manual slew for 3 seconds." They want to leave it there for temperature purposes.
This will be a cooler position for it during ascent.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And the first 01...(correcting himself) first (Noun) 04 was 0.01.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We've got you on television now.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Glad to see old Rover's still working.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) Go ahead and torque them.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: P12 looks good, and the PGNS is in Auto.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I do have a K-factor for you.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I have a couple of PIPA (Pulsed Integrating Pendulous Accelerometer) bias updates for the PGNS.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, with a Verb 21 Noun 01, load address 1452 with 03045, and load address 1454 with 05246.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We gave you the wrong skinny (slang for "information") on the comm configuration last rev.
We're actually, now, in a full MSFN relay mode.
We'd like you to stay off of VOX.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: When you get to a break point, I have some words on what you can expect in the way of guidance steering.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You can go ahead and talk while we're putting our helmets and gloves on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We never got around to debriefing you on PDI, but the out-of-plane indications you saw on the
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Pause to listen to Fullerton's last sentence) Understand the AGS on that one.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Also, I'll start pumping up the cabin, Direct O2.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: We're at lift-off minus 17 minutes, and Verb 47 going over.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: America, we see your cabin at 5.5.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Houston, the Descent Batts are coming Off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) America, it looks like, to us here, your maneuver's stopped.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: You roll around in this suit, you knock the thing off of lock.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Challenger, we're recommending PGNS direct rendezvous.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (Long Pause: static disappears after 30 seconds) America, this is Houston; voice check.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And we're standing by for lift-off minus 10 (minutes).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Houston, are you in relay now?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We lost high-gain on America, so we inhibited the downlink.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We're actually in a one-way relay.
Ron should be able to hear you, but not vice versa.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Houston; coming up on 10 minutes.
And we're ready to pressurize the APS (Ascent Propulsion System).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; you're Go to pressurize the APS (Ascent Propulsion System).
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: The Master Arm is coming On.
Okay, Ascent Helium Press(urize) Tank 1, Fire.
We got a squib fired, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) Challenger, you're Go for Tank 2.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (To Jack) Wonder why they didn't balance off?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Master Arm is coming Off, and the lights are out.
System A Main SOV (Shut Off Valve) Closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Jack) How does it look to you?
(Pause) I can feel them (the valve charges) in the floor when they go.
Okay; and B Main SOV, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And America, can you read Houston?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We're standing by for 5 (minutes to lift-off).
(Pause) Houston, Challenger is Go for lift-off.
We're at 7:54 (prior to lift-off) and counting.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We think the transducer in tank 2 has shifted.
We want you to monitor tank 1 for APS helium (pressure).
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: We were looking at that, and we'll monitor 1.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Jack, who is back on VOX) What of this do you want?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I need the big one there, with the cards in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's go over the APS burn card.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My Display/Engine Override Logic breaker is In; circuit breakers Stab(ilization)/Control all Closed on panel 11, except AEA and DECA Power.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Stab/Control breakers are all Closed, except AEA and DECA Power.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And all of mine are Closed except Descent Engine Override.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Attitude Control, three, to Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Attitude Control, three, on Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you're going to be in Auto and Att Hold.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let's take a swing around the systems.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The propellant pressure is holding up.
We're tied to the Ascent tanks on the RCS (Reaction Control System).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) And let's go to the Timeline Book.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Make sure of everything in here.
At 2 minutes, I'll get the Master Arm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll get the (16 mm sequence) camera and...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: At 10 seconds I'll hit the Abort Stage, followed by the Engine Arm to Ascent.
If we don't get a start, I'll go Guidance Control to AGS.
And if we don't get a start, we'll back off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, we have an awful lot of noise coming up (the comm circuit).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think we can read you, Gordy.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can cut it out with a high squelch (setting) on Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Challenger's at 2 minutes and 50 seconds (prior to launch).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not going to be able to squelch him (Evans) out any more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Turn the volume down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Responding to Evans, who is talking to them directly) Reading you loud and clear, America.
We're coming up on 02:10 from lift-off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Evans) We'll be with you shortly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My watch is reset (as a backup to the event timer.) (Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You want to pick up the (16 mm sequence) camera just before I hit Abort Stage.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Take your final look at the valley of Taurus-Littrow, except from orbit.
We're 50 seconds now, and we're Go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Camera's not going to run without me holding it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to get the Pro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've lost data right now, but we'd like Aft Omni.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Coming right over the top of Camelot.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you get it on an Omni, or something?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See if you can get comm back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You're loud and clear, and both systems look good.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: See if we can get comm.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're in the blind, and we're Go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like the AGS to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: I got good lock...(Still not hearing Houston) No.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Houston; coming up on 2 minutes...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like AGS to Mode Control, Auto.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: ...Challenger is Go and coming through 14K (fourteen thousand feet).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just get comm, if you can.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We need a 623 plus 10,000 in the AGS.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (To Gene) Well, those are the angles.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Three minutes, and Challenger is Go.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Evans) I tried it...I tried it, Ron, and it doesn't hold.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: All my comm breakers are in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: ...that they're right on the money on trajectory; both systems are Go.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: In the blind, Challenger's Go; coming up on 03:25 and at 30K.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: America, would you relay to Challenger to go Aft Omni?
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, they are in Aft Omni right now.
transcript.speaker: Evans

tokens: Noun 37 says we got about a 07:16 burn, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, at 04:30, I'll be about 282 coming through 41K.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: 282 is great; 41K is great, 73 is good; AGS and PGNS are right together.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to terminate Ascent Feed now.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Okay, Main SOV (Shut-Off Valve) is going On.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the reason is the mixture ratio problem....
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: ...That's just to be conservative and safe.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: And we're going 5 (minutes), and we're now out of 48K.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, burn time's going to be about 18 or 19.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The AGS has us just about in plane.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: PGNS and AGS are looking good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And PGNS says 126 on the H-dot.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your trajectory is right on the money.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Get a good shutoff time now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, it'll be 20...7:20 (into the burn) on the shutoff.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we've already terminated Ascent Feed.
You got 1300 (feet per second horizontal velocity) to go.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Next move is at 200 feet (per second).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Engine Arm, Off with 200 to go.
AGS and PGNS are right together.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We got about another minute to go, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, H-dot is rounding right out to the target.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, normal shutdown and normal trim procedures.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Give me a hack at 200 (fps horizontal velocity to go), Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Mark it, and the ascent feeds are already terminated.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, no trim, Houston; no trim.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're showing a 50 (mile) by 9.1 (mile elliptical orbit).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) And MSFN confirms that orbit (with tracking from Earth).
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: Get in the attitude for the tweak.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We're in attitude for the tweak.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Ignition is at 12 plus 12; VX is a minus 4.0 (feet per second); Y, minus 9.0; and Z, plus 1.0.
That's at 12:12; minus 4; minus 9; and plus 1.
transcript.speaker: Fullerton

tokens: That's at 12:12; minus 4; minus 9; and plus 1.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Fullerton Roger, that's a good readback.
transcript.speaker: Unknown
